Deploying to GitHub Pages</h1>\n\n<h2 id=\"option-a--let-github-build-jekyll-recommended-for-simple-sites\">Option A тАФ Let GitHub Build Jekyll (Recommended for simple sites)</h2>\n\n<p>Push to GitHub:</p>\n\n<div class=\"language-bash highlighter-rouge\"><div class=\"highlight\"><pre class=\"highlight\"><code>git remote add origin https://github.com/username/repo-name.git\ngit branch <span class=\"nt\">-M</span> main\ngit push <span class=\"nt\">-u</span> origin main\n</code></pre></div></div>\n\n<p>Then:</p>\n\n<ol>\n  <li>Go to Repository Settings</li>\n  <li>Click тАЬPagesтАЭ</li>\n  <li>Under тАЬSourceтАЭ тЖТ Select:\n    <ul>\n      <li>Branch: <code class=\"language-plaintext highlighter-rouge\">main</code></li>\n      <li>Folder: <code class=\"language-plaintext highlighter-rouge\">/ (root)</code></li>\n    </ul>\n  </li>\n  <li>Save</li>\n</ol>\n\n<p>Your site will be available at:</p>\n\n<p>User site:</p>\n<div class=\"language-plaintext highlighter-rouge\"><div class=\"highlight\"><pre class=\"highlight\"><code>https://username.github.io\n</code></pre></div></div>\n\n<p>Project site:</p>\n<div class=\"language-plaintext highlighter-rouge\"><div class=\"highlight\"><pre class=\"highlight\"><code>https://username.github.io/repo-name/\n</code></pre></div></div>\n\n<hr />\n\n<h2 id=\"option-b--deploy-built-_site-folder-advanced\">Option B тАФ Deploy Built <code class=\"language-plaintext highlighter-rouge\">_site/</code> Folder (Advanced)</h2>\n\n<p>Build locally:</p>\n\n<div class=\"language-bash highlighter-rouge\"><div class=\"highlight\"><pre class=\"highlight\"><code>bundle <span class=\"nb\">exec </span>jekyll build\n</code></pre></div></div>\n\n<p>Deploy <code class=\"language-plaintext highlighter-rouge\">_site/</code> contents to <code class=\"language-plaintext highlighter-rouge\">gh-pages</code> branch.</p>\n\n<p>Often done with GitHub Actions.</p>\n\n<hr />\n\n<h1 id=\"11-custom-domain\">11. Custom Domain</h1>\n\n<ol>\n  <li>Add a <code class=\"language-plaintext highlighter-rouge\">CNAME</code> file to project root:</li>\n</ol>\n\n<div class=\"language-text highlighter-rouge\"><div class=\"highlight\"><pre class=\"highlight\"><code>example.com\n</code></pre></div></div>\n\n<ol>\n  <li>Commit and push</li>\n  <li>In GitHub Pages settings:\n    <ul>\n      <li>Add custom domain</li>\n    </ul>\n  </li>\n  <li>Configure DNS:\n    <ul>\n      <li>A record тЖТ GitHub IPs</li>\n      <li>OR CNAME тЖТ username.github.io</li>\n    </ul>\n  </li>\n</ol>\n\n<hr />\n\n<h1 id=\"12-github-pages-plugin-limitations\">12. GitHub Pages Plugin Limitations</h1>\n\n<p>GitHub Pages only supports approved plugins.</p>\n\n<p>Supported examples:</p>\n<ul>\n  <li>jekyll-feed</li>\n  <li>jekyll-seo-tag</li>\n  <li>jekyll-sitemap</li>\n</ul>\n\n<p>If you need custom plugins:</p>\n<ul>\n  <li>Use GitHub Actions</li>\n  <li>Build site yourself</li>\n  <li>Deploy static output</li>\n</ul>\n\n<hr />\n\n<h1 id=\"13-drafts\">13. Drafts</h1>\n\n<p>Create <code class=\"language-plaintext highlighter-rouge\">_drafts/</code> folder.</p>\n\n<p>Add:</p>\n\n<div class=\"language-text highlighter-rouge\"><div class=\"highlight\"><pre class=\"highlight\"><code>_drafts/my-draft.md\n</code></pre></div></div>\n\n<p>Serve with:</p>\n\n<div class=\"language-bash highlighter-rouge\"><div class=\"highlight\"><pre class=\"highlight\"><code>bundle <span class=\"nb\">exec </span>jekyll serve <span class=\"nt\">--drafts</span>\n</code></pre></div></div>\n\n<hr />\n\n<h1 id=\"14-common-issues\">14. Common Issues</h1>\n\n<h2 id=\"broken-css-or-links\">Broken CSS or Links</h2>\n\n<p>Cause:</p>\n<ul>\n  <li>Incorrect <code class=\"language-plaintext highlighter-rouge\">baseurl</code></li>\n</ul>\n\n<p>Fix:\nUse:</p>\n\n<div class=\"language-liquid highlighter-rouge\"><div class=\"highlight\"><pre class=\"highlight\"><code>/dev-pages/assets/css/style.css\n</code></pre></div></div>\n\n<hr />\n\n<h2 id=\"404-errors\">404 Errors</h2>\n\n<p>Cause:</p>\n<ul>\n  <li>Wrong permalink</li>\n  <li>Missing trailing slash</li>\n</ul>\n\n<hr />\n\n<h2 id=\"liquid-errors\">Liquid Errors</h2>\n\n<p>Check terminal output for line numbers.</p>\n\n<hr />\n\n<h2 id=\"site-not-updating\">Site Not Updating</h2>\n\n<p>Try:</p>\n\n<div class=\"language-bash highlighter-rouge\"><div class=\"highlight\"><pre class=\"highlight\"><code>bundle <span class=\"nb\">exec </span>jekyll clean\nbundle <span class=\"nb\">exec </span>jekyll serve\n</code></pre></div></div>\n\n<hr />\n\n<h1 id=\"15-recommended-folder-structure\">15. Recommended Folder Structure</h1>\n\n<div class=\"language-text highlighter-rouge\"><div class=\"highlight\"><pre class=\"highlight\"><code>.\nтФЬтФАтФА _config.yml\nтФЬтФАтФА _layouts/\nтФЬтФАтФА _includes/\nтФЬтФАтФА _posts/\nтФЬтФАтФА _data/\nтФЬтФАтФА assets/\nтФВ   тФЬтФАтФА css/\nтФВ   тФЬтФАтФА js/\nтФВ   тФФтФАтФА img/\nтФФтФАтФА index.md\n</code></pre></div></div>\n\n<hr />\n\n<h1 id=\"16-best-practices\">16. Best Practices</h1>\n\n<ul>\n  <li>Always use <code class=\"language-plaintext highlighter-rouge\">relative_url</code></li>\n  <li>Keep layouts minimal</li>\n  <li>Use collections for custom content types</li>\n  <li>Avoid heavy plugins</li>\n  <li>Keep <code class=\"language-plaintext highlighter-rouge\">_site/</code> out of git (add to <code class=\"language-plaintext highlighter-rouge\">.gitignore</code>)</li>\n  <li>Test locally before pushing</li>\n</ul>\n\n<hr />\n\n<h1 id=\"17-quick-deployment-checklist\">17. Quick Deployment Checklist</h1>\n\n<ul class=\"task-list\">\n  <li class=\"task-list-item\"><input type=\"checkbox\" class=\"task-list-item-checkbox\" disabled=\"disabled\" />Install Ruby + Bundler</li>\n  <li class=\"task-list-item\"><input type=\"checkbox\" class=\"task-list-item-checkbox\" disabled=\"disabled\" />Create Jekyll site</li>\n  <li class=\"task-list-item\"><input type=\"checkbox\" class=\"task-list-item-checkbox\" disabled=\"disabled\" />Configure <code class=\"language-plaintext highlighter-rouge\">_config.yml</code></li>\n  <li class=\"task-list-item\"><input type=\"checkbox\" class=\"task-list-item-checkbox\" disabled=\"disabled\" />Test locally</li>\n  <li class=\"task-list-item\"><input type=\"checkbox\" class=\"task-list-item-checkbox\" disabled=\"disabled\" />Create GitHub repository</li>\n  <li class=\"task-list-item\"><input type=\"checkbox\" class=\"task-list-item-checkbox\" disabled=\"disabled\" />Push code</li>\n  <li class=\"task-list-item\"><input type=\"checkbox\" class=\"task-list-item-checkbox\" disabled=\"disabled\" />Enable GitHub Pages</li>\n  <li class=\"task-list-item\"><input type=\"checkbox\" class=\"task-list-item-checkbox\" disabled=\"disabled\" />Verify live URL</li>\n</ul>\n\n<hr />\n\n<h1 id=\"18-final-workflow-summary\">18. Final Workflow Summary</h1>\n\n<div class=\"language-text highlighter-rouge\"><div class=\"highlight\"><pre class=\"highlight\"><code>Write Markdown\n      тЖУ\nRun jekyll serve\n      тЖУ\nCommit &amp; push to GitHub\n      тЖУ\nGitHub Pages builds site\n      тЖУ\nWebsite is live\n</code></pre></div></div>\n\n<hr />\n\n<p>You now have a complete workflow for building and deploying Jekyll sites with GitHub Pages.</p>\n\n</article>\n\n        </main>\n\n      </div>\n    </div>\n\n    <footer class=\"bg-slate-100 border-t border-slate-200 mt-16 dark:bg-neutral-950 dark:border-neutral-800\">\n  <div class=\"max-w-7xl mx-auto px-4 py-10 grid md:grid-cols-3 justify-items-start gap-8 text-sm text-slate-600 dark:text-neutral-400\">\n    \n    <!-- About -->\n    <!--<div>\n      <h3 class=\"font-semibold text-slate-900 mb-3 dark:text-neutral-100\">About</h3>\n      <p>\n        A modern Markdown + Tailwind, Jekyll site hosted on Github Pages\n      </p>\n    </div>-->\n\n    <!-- Navigation -->\n    <!--<div>\n      <h3 class=\"font-semibold text-slate-900 mb-3 dark:text-neutral-100\">Navigation</h3>\n      <ul class=\"space-y-2\">\n        \n          <li>\n            <a href=\"/dev-pages/\" class=\"hover:text-slate-900 dark:hover:text-neutral-100\">\n              Home\n            </a>\n          </li>\n        \n          <li>\n            <a href=\"/dev-pages/app/\" class=\"hover:text-slate-900 dark:hover:text-neutral-100\">\n              Docs\n            </a>\n          </li>\n        \n          <li>\n            <a href=\"/dev-pages/about/\" class=\"hover:text-slate-900 dark:hover:text-neutral-100\">\n              About\n            </a>\n          </li>\n        \n          <li>\n            <a href=\"/dev-pages/contact/\" class=\"hover:text-slate-900 dark:hover:text-neutral-100\">\n              Contact\n            </a>\n          </li>\n        \n      </ul>\n    </div>-->\n\n    <!-- Social -->\n    <!--<div>\n      <h3 class=\"font-semibold text-slate-900 mb-3 dark:text-neutral-100\">Connect</h3>\n      <ul class=\"space-y-2\">\n        \n        \n        \n      </ul>\n    </div>\n\n  </div>-->\n\n  <div class=\"text-center text-xs text-slate-500 dark:text-neutral-500\">\n    ┬й <span id=\"year\"></span> Dev-pages. All rights reserved.\n  </div>\n\n  <script>\n    (function () {\n      var root = document.documentElement;\n      var toggle = document.getElementById(\"theme-toggle\");\n      var key = \"theme\";\n\n      document.getElementById(\"year\").textContent = new Date().getFullYear();\n\n      if (!toggle) return;\n\n      function setTheme(theme, persist) {\n        var dark = theme === \"dark\";\n        root.classList.toggle(\"dark\", dark);\n        root.setAttribute(\"data-theme\", dark ? \"dark\" : \"light\");\n\n        if (persist) {\n          localStorage.setItem(key, dark ? \"dark\" : \"light\");\n        }\n\n        //toggle.textContent = dark ? \"Light\" : \"Dark\";\n        toggle.setAttribute(\"aria-label\", dark ? \"Switch to light mode\" : \"Switch to dark mode\");\n      }\n\n      setTheme(root.classList.contains(\"dark\") ? \"dark\" : \"light\", false);\n\n      toggle.addEventListener(\"click\", function () {\n        setTheme(root.classList.contains(\"dark\") ? \"light\" : \"dark\", true);\n      });\n    })();\n  </script>\n</footer>\n\n\n    <div id=\"bg\" class=\"fixed inset-0 w-full h-full opacity-60\" style=\"z-index:-1\"></div>\n    <script src=\"https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/three.js/r128/three.min.js\"></script>\n    <script defer src=\"/dev-pages/assets/app/terrain.js\"></script>\n  </body>\n</html>\n","[https://jekyllrb.com/docs/](https://jekyllrb.com/docs/)\n\n\n# Jekyll Websites тАФ Overview\n\n## What Jekyll is (in one paragraph)\nJekyll is a **static site generator**: you write content in **Markdown/HTML**, configure site behavior with **YAML**, and Jekyll builds a folder of **static files** (HTML/CSS/JS) you can host anywhere (GitHub Pages, Netlify, S3, etc.). It shines for blogs, docs, and marketing sites that donтАЩt need a dynamic backend.\n\n---\n\n## Mental Model\n**Inputs**\n- Markdown posts/pages\n- Layouts and includes (Liquid templates)\n- Data files (YAML/JSON/CSV)\n- Config (`_config.yml`)\n- Assets (`assets/`)\n\n**Build**\n- Jekyll processes Markdown + Liquid + front matter\n- Generates static HTML into `_site/`\n\n**Output**\n- `_site/` is the deployable website\n\n---\n\n## Common Folder Structure\n```txt\n.\nтФЬтФА _config.yml\nтФЬтФА Gemfile\nтФЬтФА index.md (or index.html)\nтФЬтФА _layouts/\nтФВ  тФЬтФА default.html\nтФВ  тФЬтФА page.html\nтФВ  тФФтФА post.html\nтФЬтФА _includes/\nтФВ  тФЬтФА head.html\nтФВ  тФЬтФА header.html\nтФВ  тФФтФА footer.html\nтФЬтФА _posts/\nтФВ  тФФтФА 2026-02-24-my-post-title.md\nтФЬтФА _data/\nтФВ  тФФтФА navigation.yml\nтФЬтФА _sass/              (optional)\nтФЬтФА assets/\nтФВ  тФЬтФА css/\nтФВ  тФЬтФА js/\nтФВ  тФФтФА img/\nтФФтФА _site/              (generated, do not edit)\n\n\n# Key Concepts\n\n## 1) Front Matter (YAML)\n\nFront matter is YAML at the top of a Markdown/HTML file that sets metadata and controls rendering.\n\n```yaml\n---\nlayout: post\ntitle: \"Hello Jekyll\"\ndate: 2026-02-24\npermalink: /blog/hello/\ncategories: [news]\ntags: [jekyll, liquid]\n---\n```\n\n## 2) Layouts (_layouts)\n\nLayouts wrap content. A layout must include \\`\\{\\{ content \\}\\}\\` where the page/post body should appear.\n\n{% raw %}\n```html\n<!-- _layouts/default.html -->\n<!doctype html>\n<html lang=\"en\">\n  <head>{% include head.html %}</head>\n  <body>\n    {% include header.html %}\n    <main>{{ content }}</main>\n    {% include footer.html %}\n  </body>\n</html>\n```\n{% endraw %}\n## 3) Includes (_includes)\n\nReusable partials: nav, head tags, footer, etc.\n\n{% raw %}\n```liquid\n{% include header.html %}\n```\n{% endraw %}\n\n## 4) Liquid (templating language)\n\nJekyll uses Liquid for variables, loops, conditionals, and filters.\n\n---\n\n# Build & Run Cheatsheet\n\n## Install & Serve\n\n```bash\nbundle install\nbundle exec jekyll serve --livereload\n```\n\n## Build\n\n```bash\nbundle exec jekyll build\n```\n\n## Clean (remove generated files)\n\n```bash\nbundle exec jekyll clean\n```\n\n## Useful serve flags\n\n```bash\nbundle exec jekyll serve --drafts\nbundle exec jekyll serve --incremental\nbundle exec jekyll serve --host 0.0.0.0 --port 4000\n```\n\n---\n\n# _config.yml Cheatsheet\n\n{% raw %}\n```yaml\ntitle: My Site\ndescription: Notes and articles\nurl: \"https://example.com\"     # production base\nbaseurl: \"\"                    # if hosted at /subpath, set \"/subpath\"\n\nmarkdown: kramdown\npermalink: /blog/:title/       # or /:categories/:year/:month/:day/:title/\n\ntimezone: America/New_York\n\ncollections:\n  pages:\n    output: true\n\ndefaults:\n  - scope:\n      path: \"\"\n      type: posts\n    values:\n      layout: post\n\nexclude:\n  - node_modules\n  - vendor\n  - Gemfile\n  - Gemfile.lock\n```\n{% endraw %}\n\n**Tip:** `baseurl` matters a lot on GitHub Pages project sites.\n\n---\n\n# Posts Cheatsheet\n\n## Post file naming\n\n```text\n_posts/YYYY-MM-DD-title.md\n```\n\n## Post example\n\n```markdown\n---\nlayout: post\ntitle: \"My First Post\"\n---\nThis is my post body.\n```\n\n## Loop all posts\n\n{% raw %}\n```liquid\n<ul>\n  {% for post in site.posts %}\n    <li>\n      <a href=\"{{ post.url | relative_url }}\">{{ post.title }}</a>\n      <small>{{ post.date | date: \"%b %d, %Y\" }}</small>\n    </li>\n  {% endfor %}\n</ul>\n```\n{% endraw %}\n\n## Drafts\n\nPut drafts in `_drafts/` without a date prefix.\n\nServe them with:\n\n```bash\nbundle exec jekyll serve --drafts\n```\n\n---\n\n# Pages Cheatsheet\n\n## A page at /about/\n\nCreate `about.md`:\n\n```markdown\n---\nlayout: page\ntitle: About\npermalink: /about/\n---\nHello!\n```\n\n## Page listing example\n\n{% raw %}\n```liquid\n{% assign pages = site.pages | where_exp: \"p\", \"p.title\" %}\n<ul>\n  {% for p in pages %}\n    <li><a href=\"{{ p.url | relative_url }}\">{{ p.title }}</a></li>\n  {% endfor %}\n</ul>\n```\n{% endraw %}\n\n---\n\n# Collections Cheatsheet (Beyond posts)\n\n## Define a collection\n\n```yaml\ncollections:\n  projects:\n    output: true\n    permalink: /projects/:name/\n```\n\n## Create items\n\n```text\n_projects/my-project.md\n```\n\n```markdown\n---\ntitle: My Project\n---\nDetails here.\n```\n\n## List collection items\n\n{% raw %}\n```liquid\n<ul>\n  {% for item in site.projects %}\n    <li><a href=\"{{ item.url | relative_url }}\">{{ item.title }}</a></li>\n  {% endfor %}\n</ul>\n```\n{% endraw %}\n\n---\n\n# Data Files (_data) Cheatsheet\n\n## _data/navigation.yml\n\n```yaml\n- label: Home\n  url: /\n- label: Blog\n  url: /blog/\n- label: About\n  url: /about/\n```\n\n## Render navigation\n\n{% raw %}\n```liquid\n<nav>\n  <ul>\n    {% for item in site.data.navigation %}\n      <li><a href=\"{{ item.url | relative_url }}\">{{ item.label }}</a></li>\n    {% endfor %}\n  </ul>\n</nav>\n```\n{% endraw %}\n\n---\n\n# Liquid Basics Cheatsheet\n\n## Output a variable\n\n{% raw %}\n```liquid\n{{ page.title }}\n{{ site.title }}\n```\n{% endraw %}\n\n## If / else\n\n{% raw %}\n```liquid\n{% if page.layout == \"post\" %}\n  <p>Reading a post</p>\n{% else %}\n  <p>Not a post</p>\n{% endif %}\n```\n{% endraw %}\n\n## For loop\n\n{% raw %}\n```liquid\n{% for tag in page.tags %}\n  <span>{{ tag }}</span>\n{% endfor %}\n```\n{% endraw %}\n\n## Common filters\n\n{% raw %}\n```liquid\n{{ \"hello world\" | upcase }}\n{{ page.date | date: \"%Y-%m-%d\" }}\n{{ \"/assets/css/site.css\" | relative_url }}\n{{ site.url | append: page.url }}\n```\n{% endraw %}\n\n## Assign / capture\n\n{% raw %}\n```liquid\n{% assign featured = site.posts | first %}\n{{ featured.title }}\n\n{% capture hero %}\n  <h1>{{ page.title }}</h1>\n{% endcapture %}\n{{ hero }}\n```\n{% endraw %}\n\n---\n\n# URLs & Linking (the тАЬdonтАЩt break in prodтАЭ section)\n\n## Use relative_url and absolute_url\n\n{% raw %}\n```html\n<a href=\"{{ '/about/' | relative_url }}\">About</a>\n<link rel=\"stylesheet\" href=\"{{ '/assets/css/site.css' | relative_url }}\">\n\n<a href=\"{{ '/about/' | absolute_url }}\">Absolute About</a>\n```\n{% endraw %}\n\n### Rule of thumb:\n\n- Use `relative_url` for internal paths (safe with baseurl)\n- Use `absolute_url` for canonical URLs, RSS, SEO tags\n\n---\n\n# Assets & Styling Cheatsheet\n\n## Add a stylesheet\n\n{% raw %}\n```html\n<link rel=\"stylesheet\" href=\"{{ '/assets/css/site.css' | relative_url }}\">\n```\n{% endraw %}\n\n## Typical assets layout\n\n```text\nassets/css/site.css\nassets/js/site.js\nassets/img/logo.svg\n```\n\n## Sass support (optional)\n\nIf you use Sass:\n\n- Put `.scss` in `_sass/` (partials)\n- Put a main `.scss` in `assets/css/`\n\nExample main file:\n\n```scss\n---\n---\n@import \"base\";\n@import \"layout\";\n```\n\n---\n\n# SEO & Feeds (common plugins)\n\n## RSS feed (common)\n\nPlugin: `jekyll-feed`  \nAdds `/feed.xml`\n\n## SEO tags\n\nPlugin: `jekyll-seo-tag`\n\nUsage in `<head>`:\n\n{% raw %}\n```liquid\n{% seo %}\n```\n{% endraw %}\n\n---\n\n# GitHub Pages Notes (practical)\n\nGitHub Pages supports Jekyll builds, but not all plugins (unless you build elsewhere and push `_site/`).\n\nIf you need unrestricted plugins, build on CI (GitHub Actions) and deploy the static output.\n\n---\n\n# Debugging Cheatsheet\n\n## See config values\n\n```liquid\n<pre>{{ site | jsonify }}</pre>\n```\n\n## Common gotchas\n\n- Missing front matter тЖТ file wonтАЩt be processed as expected\n- baseurl mismatch тЖТ broken links/assets on GitHub Pages project sites\n- Wrong permalink тЖТ 404s\n- Liquid syntax errors тЖТ build fails; check terminal output line numbers\n- Windows line endings can sometimes cause weirdness in scripts/config\n\n---\n\n# Quick Templates (copy/paste)\n\n## _layouts/post.html\n\n{% raw %}\n```html\n---\nlayout: default\n---\n<article class=\"prose\">\n  <h1>{{ page.title }}</h1>\n  <p class=\"meta\">{{ page.date | date: \"%B %d, %Y\" }}</p>\n  {{ content }}\n</article>\n```\n{% endraw %}\n\n## Blog index page (blog.md)\n\n{% raw %}\n```markdown\n---\nlayout: page\ntitle: Blog\npermalink: /blog/\n---\n\n<ul>\n  {% for post in site.posts %}\n    <li>\n      <a href=\"{{ post.url | relative_url }}\">{{ post.title }}</a>\n      <small>{{ post.date | date: \"%b %d, %Y\" }}</small>\n    </li>\n  {% endfor %}\n</ul>\n```\n\n## Simple nav include (_includes/header.html)\n\n```html\n<header>\n  <a href=\"{{ '/' | relative_url }}\">{{ site.title }}</a>\n  <nav>\n    <a href=\"{{ '/blog/' | relative_url }}\">Blog</a>\n    <a href=\"{{ '/about/' | relative_url }}\">About</a>\n  </nav>\n</header>\n```\n{% endraw %}\n\n---\n\n# Handy Reference: Variables YouтАЩll Use Most\n\n## Site-level\n\n- site.title, site.description\n- site.url, site.baseurl\n- site.posts\n- site.pages\n- site.data.*\n- site.collections\n\n## Page/post-level\n\n- page.title, page.url, page.permalink\n- page.date, page.categories, page.tags\n- content (only inside layouts)\n\n---\n\n# Checklist: Starting a New Jekyll Site\n\n- Create `_config.yml` with url and baseurl\n- Add `_layouts/default.html` with \\'\\{\\{ content \\}\\}\\'\n- Add `_includes/head.html`, header.html, footer.html\n- Create `index.md`\n- Add first post in `_posts/`\n- Serve locally with `bundle exec jekyll serve --livereload`\n- Deploy `_site/` or let GitHub Pages build (depending on plugin needs)","[https://www.markdownguide.org/basic-syntax/](https://www.markdownguide.org/basic-syntax/)\n\n## Markdown Cheatsheet\n\nA quick, practical reference for **writing Markdown** (GitHub-flavored where applicable).\n\n---\n\n## 1. Headings\n\n```md\n# H1\n## H2\n### H3\n#### H4\n##### H5\n###### H6\n```\n\n---\n\n## 2. Text Formatting\n\n```md\n**Bold**\n*Italic*\n***Bold & Italic***\n~~Strikethrough~~\n`Inline code`\n```\n\nResult:\n- **Bold**\n- *Italic*\n- ***Bold & Italic***\n- ~~Strikethrough~~\n- `Inline code`\n\n---\n\n## 3. Paragraphs & Line Breaks\n\n```md\nThis is a paragraph.\n\nThis is another paragraph.\n```\n\nLine break (GitHub-flavored): end a line with two spaces.\n\n---\n\n## 4. Lists\n\n### Unordered\n```md\n- Item\n- Item\n  - Nested item\n```\n\n### Ordered\n```md\n1. First\n2. Second\n3. Third\n```\n\n### Task Lists (GitHub)\n```md\n- [x] Done\n- [ ] Todo\n```\n\n---\n\n## 5. Links\n\n```md\n[Link text](https://example.com)\n```\n\n### Auto Links\n```md\nhttps://example.com\n```\n\n---\n\n## 6. Images\n\n```md\n![Alt text](image.png)\n```\n\n---\n\n## 7. Code Blocks\n\n### Fenced Code Block\n```md\n```js\nconsole.log('Hello');\n```\n```\n\n### Inline Code\n```md\nUse `npm install`\n```\n\n---\n\n## 8. Blockquotes\n\n```md\n> This is a quote\n>> Nested quote\n```\n\n---\n\n## 9. Horizontal Rules\n\n```md\n---\n***\n___\n```\n\n---\n\n## 10. Tables (GitHub)\n\n```md\n| Name | Age | Role |\n|------|-----|------|\n| Sam  | 30  | Dev  |\n| Lee  | 28  | PM   |\n```\nResult:  \n<br>\n| Name | Age | Role |\n|------|-----|------|\n| Sam  | 30  | Dev  |\n| Lee  | 28  | PM   |  \n  \n<br>\n\nAlignment:\n```md\n| Left | Center | Right |\n|:-----|:------:|------:|\n```\n\n---\n\n## 11. Escaping Characters\n\n```md\n\\* \\_ \\# \\`\n```\n\n---\n\n## 12. HTML in Markdown\n\n```md\n<b>Bold</b>\n<br />\n```\n\n---\n\n## 13. Emojis (GitHub)\n\n```md\n:rocket: :sparkles: :bug:\n```\n\n---\n\n## 14. Footnotes (Some Parsers)\n\n```md\nHere is a footnote reference[^1].\n\n[^1]: Footnote text\n```\n\n---\n\n## 15. Collapsible Sections (GitHub)\n\n```md\n<details>\n  <summary>Click to expand</summary>\n\n  Hidden content\n</details>\n```\nResult:  \n<br>\n<details>\n  <summary>Click to expand</summary>\n\n  Hidden content\n</details>  \n<br>\n\n---\n\n## 16. File Extensions\n\n- `.md`\n- `.markdown`\n\n---\n\n## 17. Common Use Cases\n\n- README files\n- Documentation\n- GitHub issues & PRs\n- Notes & wikis\n\n---\n\n## 18. Markdown Gotchas\n\n- Blank lines matter\n- Indentation affects lists & code blocks\n- Not all parsers support all features\n\n---\n\n## 19. GitHub-Flavored Markdown Extras\n\n- Tables\n- Task lists\n- Strikethrough\n- Syntax highlighting\n\n---\n\n**Tip:** Keep Markdown simpleтАФreadability beats clever formatting.\n\n","[https://tailwindcss.com/docs/installation/framework-guides/nextjs](https://tailwindcss.com/docs/installation/framework-guides/nextjs)\n\n<iframe src=\"https://www.youtube.com/embed/Xb8ajtvFLOk?si=bU8wsFIFT54OVk6B\" title=\"YouTube video player\" frameborder=\"0\" allow=\"accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share\" referrerpolicy=\"strict-origin-when-cross-origin\" allowfullscreen></iframe>\n\n## Tailwind CSS Cheatsheet\n\nA practical reference for common **Tailwind CSS** utility classes (Tailwind v3+).\n\n---\n\n## 1. Layout\n\n### Container\n\n```html\n<div class=\"container mx-auto px-4\"></div>\n```\n\n### Display\n\n```md\nblock | inline-block | inline | flex | inline-flex | grid | hidden\n```\n\n```html\n<div class=\"flex\"></div>\n<div class=\"grid\"></div>\n```\n\n### Position\n\n```md\nstatic | relative | absolute | fixed | sticky\n```\n\n```html\n<div class=\"relative\">\n  <div class=\"absolute top-0 right-0\"></div>\n</div>\n```\n\n### Z-Index\n\n```html\n<div class=\"z-10\"></div>\n```\n\n---\n\n## 2. Flexbox\n\n### Direction & Wrap\n\n```html\n<div class=\"flex flex-row flex-wrap\"></div>\n<div class=\"flex flex-col\"></div>\n```\n\n### Align Items / Justify\n\n```html\n<div class=\"items-start items-center items-end\"></div>\n<div class=\"justify-start justify-center justify-between justify-end\"></div>\n```\n\n### Gap\n\n```html\n<div class=\"gap-2 gap-4 gap-8\"></div>\n```\n\n### Flex Sizing\n\n```html\n<div class=\"flex-1\"></div>\n<div class=\"flex-none\"></div>\n<div class=\"grow\"></div>\n<div class=\"shrink-0\"></div>\n```\n\n---\n\n## 3. Grid\n\n### Columns / Rows\n\n```html\n<div class=\"grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3\"></div>\n<div class=\"grid grid-rows-2\"></div>\n```\n\n### Spans\n\n```html\n<div class=\"col-span-2 row-span-2\"></div>\n```\n\n### Gap\n\n```html\n<div class=\"gap-4\"></div>\n```\n\n---\n\n## 4. Spacing\n\n### Padding / Margin\n\n```html\n<div class=\"p-4 px-6 py-2\"></div>\n<div class=\"m-4 mx-auto my-2\"></div>\n```\n\n### Space Between (children)\n\n```html\n<div class=\"space-y-4\"></div>\n<div class=\"space-x-2\"></div>\n```\n\n---\n\n## 5. Sizing\n\n### Width / Height\n\n```html\n<div class=\"w-full h-screen\"></div>\n<div class=\"w-64 h-48\"></div>\n<div class=\"min-h-screen\"></div>\n<div class=\"max-w-3xl\"></div>\n```\n\n### Aspect Ratio\n\n```html\n<div class=\"aspect-video\"></div>\n```\n\n---\n\n## 6. Typography\n\n### Font Size / Weight\n\n```html\n<p class=\"text-sm text-base text-lg text-xl text-2xl\"></p>\n<p class=\"font-normal font-medium font-semibold font-bold\"></p>\n```\n\n### Line Height / Tracking\n\n```html\n<p class=\"leading-tight leading-normal leading-relaxed\"></p>\n<p class=\"tracking-tight tracking-wide\"></p>\n```\n\n### Alignment\n\n```html\n<p class=\"text-left text-center text-right\"></p>\n```\n\n### Text Transform\n\n```html\n<p class=\"uppercase lowercase capitalize\"></p>\n```\n\n### Truncation\n\n```html\n<p class=\"truncate\"></p>\n<p class=\"line-clamp-2\"></p>\n```\n\n---\n\n## 7. Colors\n\n### Text / Background\n\n```html\n<div class=\"text-gray-900 bg-gray-100\"></div>\n<div class=\"text-white bg-blue-600\"></div>\n```\n\n### Opacity\n\n```html\n<div class=\"bg-black/50\"></div>\n<div class=\"text-white/80\"></div>\n```\n\n---\n\n## 8. Borders & Radius\n\n### Border\n\n```html\n<div class=\"border border-gray-200\"></div>\n<div class=\"border-t border-b\"></div>\n```\n\n### Radius\n\n```html\n<div class=\"rounded rounded-md rounded-lg rounded-xl rounded-2xl\"></div>\n<div class=\"rounded-full\"></div>\n```\n\n---\n\n## 9. Shadows\n\n```html\n<div class=\"shadow shadow-md shadow-lg shadow-xl\"></div>\n```\n\n---\n\n## 10. Backgrounds\n\n### Gradients\n\n```html\n<div class=\"bg-gradient-to-r from-indigo-500 via-purple-500 to-pink-500\"></div>\n```\n\n### Background Image / Cover\n\n```html\n<div class=\"bg-cover bg-center\"></div>\n```\n\n---\n\n## 11. Effects\n\n### Opacity\n\n```html\n<div class=\"opacity-0 opacity-50 opacity-100\"></div>\n```\n\n### Blur\n\n```html\n<div class=\"blur-sm blur-md blur-lg\"></div>\n```\n\n---\n\n## 12. Transitions & Animation\n\n### Transition\n\n```html\n<button class=\"transition duration-200 ease-in-out hover:scale-105\"></button>\n```\n\n### Common\n\n```html\n<div class=\"transition-colors\"></div>\n<div class=\"transition-transform\"></div>\n```\n\n---\n\n## 13. Hover / Focus / Active\n\n```html\n<button class=\"bg-blue-600 hover:bg-blue-700 focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-400 active:scale-95\">\n  Button\n</button>\n```\n\n---\n\n## 14. Responsive Breakpoints\n\n```md\nsm: 640px\nmd: 768px\nlg: 1024px\nxl: 1280px\n2xl: 1536px\n```\n\nExample:\n\n```html\n<div class=\"p-2 md:p-6 lg:p-10\"></div>\n```\n\n---\n\n## 15. Dark Mode\n\n```html\n<div class=\"bg-white text-gray-900 dark:bg-gray-900 dark:text-gray-100\"></div>\n```\n\n---\n\n## 16. State Variants\n\n```md\nhover: focus: active: disabled:\nfirst: last: odd: even:\npeer-*, group-*\n```\n\n### Group Hover\n\n```html\n<div class=\"group\">\n  <span class=\"group-hover:underline\">Hover parent</span>\n</div>\n```\n\n### Peer\n\n```html\n<input class=\"peer\" />\n<p class=\"hidden peer-focus:block\">Shows on focus</p>\n```\n\n---\n\n## 17. Forms\n\n```html\n<input class=\"w-full rounded-md border border-gray-300 px-3 py-2 focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-400\" />\n```\n\n---\n\n## 18. Common UI Patterns\n\n### Card\n\n```html\n<div class=\"rounded-2xl border border-gray-200 bg-white p-6 shadow-md\">\n  <h3 class=\"text-lg font-semibold\">Title</h3>\n  <p class=\"mt-2 text-sm text-gray-600\">Body text</p>\n</div>\n```\n\n### Button\n\n```html\n<button class=\"inline-flex items-center justify-center rounded-md bg-blue-600 px-4 py-2 text-white hover:bg-blue-700 active:scale-95 transition\">\n  Click\n</button>\n```\n\n### Input + Label\n\n```html\n<label class=\"block text-sm font-medium text-gray-700\">Email</label>\n<input class=\"mt-1 w-full rounded-md border border-gray-300 px-3 py-2\" />\n```\n\n---\n\n## 19. Customization\n\n### Arbitrary Values\n\n```html\n<div class=\"w-[420px] top-[3.25rem] bg-[#0f172a]\"></div>\n```\n\n### Extend in tailwind.config\n\n```js\n// theme.extend: colors, spacing, fonts, etc.\n```\n\n---\n\n## 20. Gotchas & Tips\n\n* Prefer `gap-*` over margins for spacing in flex/grid.\n* Use `container mx-auto` for centered layouts.\n* Use `prose` (Typography plugin) for rich text.\n* Use `clsx`/`cn` helper for conditional classes.\n\n---\n\n**Tip:** Build by composing utilities; extract components when repetition shows up.\n","This post was added directly from Github. Once commited, the workfow automatically runs and publishes the new page.\n","[https://docs.github.com/en/repositories/configuring-branches-and-merges-in-your-repository/configuring-pull-request-merges/about-merge-methods-on-github](https://docs.github.com/en/repositories/configuring-branches-and-merges-in-your-repository/configuring-pull-request-merges/about-merge-methods-on-github)\n\n# Adding a Commit Message When Using `git merge`\n\n---\n\n## тЬЕ Basic Syntax\n\n```bash\ngit merge branch-name -m \"Merge branch-name into dev\"\n```\n\nThis command:\n\n- Performs the merge  \n- Uses your provided commit message  \n- Skips opening the default editor (like Vim)  \n\n---\n\n## ЁЯУЭ Multi-line Commit Message\n\nYou can include a commit body by stacking multiple `-m` flags:\n\n```bash\ngit merge branch-name \\\n  -m \"Merge branch-name into dev\" \\\n  -m \"Adds new banner component, styles, and CMS schema updates.\"\n```\n\n- The first `-m` becomes the **commit title**\n- The second `-m` becomes the **commit body**\n\n---\n\n## тЪа Important Notes\n\n- The `-m` flag only applies if Git creates a **merge commit**.\n- If the merge is a **fast-forward**, Git will not create a merge commit тАФ so no message will be used.\n\nIf you want to **force a merge commit** (even when fast-forward is possible), use:\n\n```bash\ngit merge --no-ff branch-name -m \"Merge branch-name into dev\"\n```\n\n### Why use `--no-ff`?\n\n- Preserves branch history\n- Makes feature merges clearly visible in the commit log\n- Cleaner for release tracking\n- Common in team workflows\n\n---\n\n## ЁЯЪА Example: Standard Team Workflow\n\n```bash\ngit checkout dev\ngit pull origin dev\ngit merge --no-ff branch-name -m \"Merge branch-name into dev\"\ngit push origin dev\n```\n\nThis ensures:\n\n1. `dev` is up to date\n2. The merge creates a visible merge commit\n3. The updated branch is pushed to remote\n\n---\n\nIf you'd like, you can also explore:\n\n- Using `--squash` merges\n- Rebasing instead of merging\n- Best practices for CI/CD pipelines","# Vue.js + Jekyll Overview (Hybrid Static + SPA)\n\n\nThis approach lets you keep **Jekyll** as your static-site generator (posts, pages, layouts) while adding a **Vue 3 single-page app (SPA)** that lives at a dedicated path (e.g. `/app/`) and can consume Jekyll-generated content via JSON. You get:\n\n- **Static Jekyll routes** (SEO-friendly, normal page loads):  \n  `/dev-pages/docs/my-post/`\n- **SPA Vue routes** (dynamic UI, client routing):  \n  `/dev-pages/app/#/post/my-post`\n\nThe key is to use **hash routing** in Vue (`#/...`) so it works on static hosting (GitHub Pages) without server rewrites.\n\n---\n\n## Architecture Options\n\n### 1) Hybrid (Recommended)\n- Jekyll renders the main site + canonical content pages.\n- Vue SPA is mounted on a single Jekyll page (`/app/`).\n- Vue fetches content from a Jekyll-generated JSON feed (`/posts.json`).\n\nтЬЕ Best for: тАЬBoth static pages AND a true SPA in the same repo.тАЭ\n\n### 2) Vue-enhanced Jekyll (Not a full SPA)\n- Keep all routes as Jekyll pages.\n- Use Vue only for components/widgets inside pages.\n\nтЬЕ Best for: interactive UI without client routing.\n\n---\n\n## Successful Process (Brief Outline)\n\n### 1) Configure Jekyll paths\n- Set `baseurl` for GitHub Pages project sites (example):\n  - `baseurl: \"/dev-pages\"`\n- Set permalinks for post URLs (example):\n  - `permalink: /docs/:title/`\n\n### 2) Add a SPA host page in Jekyll\nCreate `/app/` as a real Jekyll page that mounts Vue:\n\n- `app/index.html` тЖТ permalink `/app/`\n- Contains `<div id=\"app\"></div>`\n- Loads the Vue entry module: `assets/app/main.js`\n\nResult:\n- Visiting `/dev-pages/app/` returns real HTML (Jekyll layout + SPA mount point)\n\n### 3) Export Jekyll content as JSON for Vue\nGenerate a JSON feed from `_posts` so the SPA can fetch content:\n\n- `posts.json` with:\n  - `title`, `date`, `slug`, `url`, `content`, etc.\n- Use `relative_url` so Jekyll automatically prefixes `baseurl`\n\nResult:\n- `/dev-pages/posts.json` becomes the SPAтАЩs content API.\n\n### 4) Use Vue 3 + Vue Router with hash routing\nInside `assets/app/main.js`:\n- Use Vue Router with `createWebHashHistory()`\n- Define routes like:\n  - `/` (SPA home)\n  - `/post/:slug` (SPA post view)\n\nResult:\n- SPA routes look like `/dev-pages/app/#/post/my-post`\n- No server rewrite rules required.\n\n### 5) Handle baseurl correctly (critical)\nEnsure fetch calls and static links include the base path:\n\n- Provide baseurl to JS via a global (e.g. `window.__BASEURL__ = \"{{ site.baseurl }}\"`)\n- Fetch JSON using:\n  - `${BASE}/posts.json`\n\nResult:\n- Works the same locally and on GitHub Pages project sites.\n\n### 6) Use import maps or CDN ESM to avoid bundlers (optional but common)\nIf you are not using Vite:\n- Use an `importmap` in `app/index.html` for:\n  - `vue`\n  - `vue-router`\n- Prefer a browser-ready Vue build:\n  - `vue.esm-browser.js`\n\nResult:\n- No Node build step required; the browser can load modules directly.\n\n### 7) Render posts in Vue from the JSON feed\nIn the SPA:\n- Home view fetches `posts.json` and lists items\n- Post view matches `:slug`, finds the post, and renders:\n  - `v-html=\"post.content\"` (Jekyll already converted Markdown тЖТ HTML)\n\nResult:\n- SPA shows the same content as JekyllтАЩs static pages, but with dynamic routing/UI.\n\n---\n\n## Resulting URL Behavior\n\n### Static (Jekyll)\n- `/dev-pages/docs/my-post/` тЖТ full HTML page rendered by Jekyll\n\n### SPA (Vue)\n- `/dev-pages/app/#/post/my-post` тЖТ Vue renders content client-side\n\nThese coexist cleanly because:\n- Jekyll owns `/docs/...`\n- Vue owns hash routes under `/app/`\n\n---\n\n## Recommended Next Steps\n- Add a second feed for metadata-only lists (faster load)\n- Add client-side search (index posts at build time)\n- Add a shared тАЬcanonical linkтАЭ:\n  - SPA page links back to the static Jekyll URL (`post.url`)\n- If the SPA grows, consider switching to Vite for:\n  - bundling, smaller payloads, better DX","# GIT command-line instructions for publishing a branch to dev, staging, and prod environments\n\n---\n\nAfter setting up GIT locally and adding remote origin, use these steps for creating a branch and publishing edits to the various environments.\n\n## Create new branch from 'main'\n\nPull updates from repository:\n\n```bash\ngit switch main\ngit pull origin main\n```\n\nThese commands:\n- Switch to the main branch  \n- Pull branch from origin to update \n\n---\n\n## Create new branch and make new edits locally\n\nCreate a branch and run dev locally. Make any edits on this branch and commit changes when finished.\n\n```bash\ngit checkout -b new-branch-name\n```\n\nThis command:\n- Creates a new branch named \"new-branch-name\"\n\n```bash\nnpm run storybook\nnpm run dev\n```\n\nThese commands:\n- Runs Storybook locally for component previewing and development\n- Runs the site locally for development\n\nAfter edits are completed, add files to GIT and commit the changes to the branch.\n\nFirst, double check to make sure you are on the correct branch:\n\n```bash\ngit status\n```\n\nThen:\n\n```bash\ngit status\ngit add .\ngit commit -m \"Message for commit\"\n```\n\nThese commands:\n- Adds all edited files to the commit log\n- Commits the edits to the current branch\n\nNow the branch is ready to push or merge.\n\n\n---\n\n## Publishing to dev (merge)\n\nTo publish to dev, the branch just needs to be merged with the \"dev\" branch. The dev project workflow is set up to publish any time edits are pushed to the dev branch (GitHub Actions). The process usually takes 5-10 minutes to complete once merged.\n\nFirst, once again, switch to the main branch and pull any updates from the remote origin:\n\n```bash\ngit switch main\ngit pull origin main\n```\n\nThen switch to the dev branch and merge the new branch into it. Then push dev to the remote origin to run the workflow:\n\n```bash\ngit switch dev\ngit merge --no-ff new-branch-name -m \"Merge branch-name into dev\"\ngit push origin dev\n```\n\nThese commands:\n- Switch to the dev branch\n- Merges edits from new-branch-name\n- pushes the updated dev branch to the remote origin\n\n### Why use `--no-ff`?\n\n- Preserves branch history\n- Makes feature merges clearly visible in the commit log\n- Cleaner for release tracking\n- Common in team workflows\n\n---\n\n## Publishing to staging\n\nTo publish to staging, the new branch must be pushed to the remote origin and have a Pull Request added. Once the Pull Request is merged with the main branch, the staging publish workflow will be triggered.\n\nSwitch to the new branch or confirm that your are currently on it:\n\n```bash\ngit switch new-branch-name\ngit status\ngit push origin new-branch-name\n```\n\nOnce the branch has been uploaded to the remote repository, navigate to Github and follow the steps to create and merge a pull request...\n\n---\n\n## Publishing to prod\n\nUse the Marketing CI workflow to publish main branch to prod:\n\nBranch: main\nDeployment environment: prod\n\nRun Workflow\n\n* workflows can be cancelled in process once they begin running\n\n\n## Rolling back changes\n\nTo roll back to a previous commit:\n\n- Find the commit hash using\n\n```bash\ngit log --oneline\n```\n\n- THen reset the main brnach using:\n\n```bash\ngit reset --hard <commit-hash>\n```","# Adding or updating redirects in Sanity/Nextjs\n\n---\n\nRedirects are added through Sanity Studio and published via Github Workflow\n\n## Add Redirect in Studio\n\nAdd redirect in Studio > Redirects and Publish:\n\n---\n\n## Create a new branch for the PR\n\nIn GIT, switch to main branch and pull the most recent code from the remote origin\n\n```bash\ngit checkout main\ngit pull origin main\n```\n\nThen create a new branch\n\n```bash\ngit checkout -b update-redirect-name\n```\n\nAdd a comment to the bottom of the next.config file to note the redirect added and have an edit to commit to the branch.\n\n```\n// Redirect added for /some-redirect/\n```\n\nSave and commit:\n\n```bash\ngit add .\ngit commit -m \"Update redirect note\"\n```\n\n## Push the update-redirects branch to remote and create PR\n\nPush the update-redirect-name branch to remote\n\n```bash\ngit push origin update-redirect-name\n```\n\nNext, navigate to Github to create a PR and Merge--this will trigger the Staging workflow--this usually takes 5-10 minutes.\n\nTest redirect in Staging.\n\n\n## Publishing to Prod\n\nUse the Marketing CI workflow to publish main branch to prod:\n\nBranch: main\nDeployment environment: prod\n\nRun Workflow\n\n* workflows can be cancelled in process once they begin running","Node conversion script for converting Sanity JSON data to CSV for import to Webflow Collection\n\n---\n\n# Sanity тЖТ Webflow Blog Migration Script\n\nThis script migrates blog content from a **Sanity dataset (API or local export)** into a format compatible with **Webflow CMS import (CSV/JSON)**.\n\nIt supports:\n\n* Full blog content (title, slug, summary, etc.)\n* Rich `pageBuilder` тЖТ HTML conversion\n* Image handling (including local assets + filenames)\n* SEO field extraction and flattening\n* Preview mode for safe testing\n* Optional asset URL transformation\n\n---\n\n# Features\n\n## Content Migration\n\n* Converts Sanity `blog` documents into Webflow-ready fields\n* Preserves:\n\n  * Title, slug, summary\n  * Author + topics\n  * Publish date\n  * Featured image\n\n## Page Builder тЖТ HTML\n\nSupports:\n\n* `row` тЖТ rich text\n* `fullWidthImage`\n* `table`\n* `section-heading`\n* `embedCode`\n* graceful fallback for unsupported blocks\n\n## Image Handling\n\n* Resolves images from:\n\n  * API (`asset->url`)\n  * local dataset (`_sanityAsset`)\n* Maps images using `assets.json`\n* Injects:\n\n```html\n<img src=\"...\" data-file-name=\"image.png\" />\n```\n\n## SEO Support\n\nFlattens nested Sanity `seo` object into Webflow fields:\n\n* Meta title / description\n* Keywords\n* No-follow flag\n* Open Graph (title, description, image, etc.)\n* Twitter fields\n* Additional meta tags (JSON)\n\n## Preview Mode\n\nInspect output without writing files:\n\n* HTML preview\n* SEO fields\n* CSV structure\n\n---\n\n# Installation\n\n```bash\nnpm install\n```\n\nRequired dependencies:\n\n* `@sanity/client`\n* `@portabletext/to-html`\n* `json2csv`\n* `dotenv`\n\n---\n\n# тЪЩя╕П Environment Variables\n\nCreate a `.env.local` file (only required for API mode):\n\n```env\nSANITY_PROJECT_ID=your_project_id\nSANITY_DATASET=production\nSANITY_API_VERSION=2025-01-01\nSANITY_TOKEN=your_token_if_needed\n```\n\n---\n\n# Local Dataset Structure\n\nWhen using a local export:\n\n```\n/export/\n  data.ndjson\n  assets.json\n  /images/\n    <asset-files>\n```\n\n### Required files:\n\n* `data.ndjson` тЖТ Sanity export\n* `assets.json` тЖТ asset metadata (IMPORTANT)\n* `/images/` тЖТ downloaded image files\n\n---\n\n# тЦ╢я╕П Usage\n\n## 1. Preview a Single Post (Recommended First Step)\n\n```bash\nnode migrate-sanity-blog-to-webflow.mjs \\\n  --local-file=./export/data.ndjson \\\n  --assets-file=./export/assets.json \\\n  --images-dir=./export/images \\\n  --slug=your-post-slug \\\n  --preview \\\n  --no-write\n```\n\n---\n\n## 2. Export a Single Post\n\n```bash\nnode migrate-sanity-blog-to-webflow.mjs \\\n  --local-file=./export/data.ndjson \\\n  --assets-file=./export/assets.json \\\n  --images-dir=./export/images \\\n  --slug=your-post-slug\n```\n\n---\n\n## 3. Export All Posts\n\n```bash\nnode migrate-sanity-blog-to-webflow.mjs \\\n  --local-file=./export/data.ndjson \\\n  --assets-file=./export/assets.json \\\n  --images-dir=./export/images\n```\n\n---\n\n## 4. Use Sanity API Instead of Local Data\n\n```bash\nnode migrate-sanity-blog-to-webflow.mjs \\\n  --slug=your-post-slug \\\n  --preview\n```\n\n---\n\n# ЁЯзк CLI Flags\n\n| Flag               | Description                        |\n| ------------------ | ---------------------------------- |\n| `--local-file`     | Path to `data.ndjson`              |\n| `--assets-file`    | Path to `assets.json`              |\n| `--images-dir`     | Path to images folder              |\n| `--slug`           | Filter by slug                     |\n| `--id`             | Filter by Sanity `_id`             |\n| `--title`          | Filter by title                    |\n| `--preview`        | Print preview instead of exporting |\n| `--no-write`       | Prevent file output                |\n| `--asset-base-url` | Replace Sanity CDN URL             |\n\n---\n\n# Asset URL Transformation\n\nOptional: replace Sanity CDN URLs\n\n```bash\n--asset-base-url=https://assets.example.com/images\n```\n\nTransforms:\n\n```\nhttps://cdn.sanity.io/images/project/dataset/file.png\nтЖУ\nhttps://assets.example.com/images/file.png\n```\n\n---\n\n# Output\n\nFiles are written to:\n\n```\n/output/\n  webflow-blog-import-<slug>.json\n  webflow-blog-import-<slug>.csv\n```\n\n---\n\n# Webflow Fields Included\n\n## Core Fields\n\n* Name\n* Slug\n* Summary\n* Body (HTML)\n* Author\n* Topics\n* Published Date\n\n## Images\n\n* Featured Image\n* Featured Image Alt\n* Featured Image File Name\n\n## SEO Fields\n\n* SEO Meta Title\n* SEO Meta Description\n* SEO Keywords\n* SEO No Follow\n* SEO Open Graph Title\n* SEO Open Graph Description\n* SEO Open Graph URL\n* SEO Open Graph Site Name\n* SEO Open Graph Image\n* SEO Open Graph Image File Name\n* SEO Twitter Handle\n* SEO Twitter Creator\n* SEO Twitter Site\n* SEO Twitter Card Type\n* SEO Additional Meta Tags JSON\n\n---\n\n# тЪая╕П Important Notes\n\n## 1. assets.json is Required for Filenames\n\nWithout it:\n\n* `data-file-name` will be empty\n* image mapping may break\n\n---\n\n## 2. Relative vs Absolute Image URLs\n\nIf using:\n\n```\n--asset-base-url=/relative-path\n```\n\nMake sure your final site serves images from that path.\n\n---\n\n## 3. Webflow Rich Text Limits\n\nVery large blog posts may exceed Webflow limits.\n\n---\n\n## 4. Unsupported Blocks\n\nSome blocks (e.g. `button-insert`) are exported as placeholders:\n\n```\n[Button Insert module requires manual mapping]\n```\n\n---\n\n# Recommended Workflow\n\n1. Run preview on 1 post\n2. Verify:\n\n   * HTML structure\n   * images\n   * SEO fields\n3. Import into Webflow\n4. Validate rendering\n5. Batch migrate remaining posts\n\n---\n\n# ЁЯПБ Summary\n\nThis script provides a complete pipeline for:\n\n* Sanity тЖТ structured HTML\n* Asset resolution (API + local)\n* SEO flattening\n* Webflow-ready export\n\nIt is designed to be:\n\n* safe (preview mode)\n* flexible (API or local)\n* extensible (custom mappings)\n\n---\n"],"related_posts":["Node conversion script for converting Sanity JSON data to CSV for import to Webflow Collection\n\n---\n\n# Sanity тЖТ Webflow Blog Migration Script\n\nThis script migrates blog content from a **Sanity dataset (API or local export)** into a format compatible with **Webflow CMS import (CSV/JSON)**.\n\nIt supports:\n\n* Full blog content (title, slug, summary, etc.)\n* Rich `pageBuilder` тЖТ HTML conversion\n* Image handling (including local assets + filenames)\n* SEO field extraction and flattening\n* Preview mode for safe testing\n* Optional asset URL transformation\n\n---\n\n# Features\n\n## Content Migration\n\n* Converts Sanity `blog` documents into Webflow-ready fields\n* Preserves:\n\n  * Title, slug, summary\n  * Author + topics\n  * Publish date\n  * Featured image\n\n## Page Builder тЖТ HTML\n\nSupports:\n\n* `row` тЖТ rich text\n* `fullWidthImage`\n* `table`\n* `section-heading`\n* `embedCode`\n* graceful fallback for unsupported blocks\n\n## Image Handling\n\n* Resolves images from:\n\n  * API (`asset->url`)\n  * local dataset (`_sanityAsset`)\n* Maps images using `assets.json`\n* Injects:\n\n```html\n<img src=\"...\" data-file-name=\"image.png\" />\n```\n\n## SEO Support\n\nFlattens nested Sanity `seo` object into Webflow fields:\n\n* Meta title / description\n* Keywords\n* No-follow flag\n* Open Graph (title, description, image, etc.)\n* Twitter fields\n* Additional meta tags (JSON)\n\n## Preview Mode\n\nInspect output without writing files:\n\n* HTML preview\n* SEO fields\n* CSV structure\n\n---\n\n# Installation\n\n```bash\nnpm install\n```\n\nRequired dependencies:\n\n* `@sanity/client`\n* `@portabletext/to-html`\n* `json2csv`\n* `dotenv`\n\n---\n\n# тЪЩя╕П Environment Variables\n\nCreate a `.env.local` file (only required for API mode):\n\n```env\nSANITY_PROJECT_ID=your_project_id\nSANITY_DATASET=production\nSANITY_API_VERSION=2025-01-01\nSANITY_TOKEN=your_token_if_needed\n```\n\n---\n\n# Local Dataset Structure\n\nWhen using a local export:\n\n```\n/export/\n  data.ndjson\n  assets.json\n  /images/\n    <asset-files>\n```\n\n### Required files:\n\n* `data.ndjson` тЖТ Sanity export\n* `assets.json` тЖТ asset metadata (IMPORTANT)\n* `/images/` тЖТ downloaded image files\n\n---\n\n# тЦ╢я╕П Usage\n\n## 1. Preview a Single Post (Recommended First Step)\n\n```bash\nnode migrate-sanity-blog-to-webflow.mjs \\\n  --local-file=./export/data.ndjson \\\n  --assets-file=./export/assets.json \\\n  --images-dir=./export/images \\\n  --slug=your-post-slug \\\n  --preview \\\n  --no-write\n```\n\n---\n\n## 2. Export a Single Post\n\n```bash\nnode migrate-sanity-blog-to-webflow.mjs \\\n  --local-file=./export/data.ndjson \\\n  --assets-file=./export/assets.json \\\n  --images-dir=./export/images \\\n  --slug=your-post-slug\n```\n\n---\n\n## 3. Export All Posts\n\n```bash\nnode migrate-sanity-blog-to-webflow.mjs \\\n  --local-file=./export/data.ndjson \\\n  --assets-file=./export/assets.json \\\n  --images-dir=./export/images\n```\n\n---\n\n## 4. Use Sanity API Instead of Local Data\n\n```bash\nnode migrate-sanity-blog-to-webflow.mjs \\\n  --slug=your-post-slug \\\n  --preview\n```\n\n---\n\n# ЁЯзк CLI Flags\n\n| Flag               | Description                        |\n| ------------------ | ---------------------------------- |\n| `--local-file`     | Path to `data.ndjson`              |\n| `--assets-file`    | Path to `assets.json`              |\n| `--images-dir`     | Path to images folder              |\n| `--slug`           | Filter by slug                     |\n| `--id`             | Filter by Sanity `_id`             |\n| `--title`          | Filter by title                    |\n| `--preview`        | Print preview instead of exporting |\n| `--no-write`       | Prevent file output                |\n| `--asset-base-url` | Replace Sanity CDN URL             |\n\n---\n\n# Asset URL Transformation\n\nOptional: replace Sanity CDN URLs\n\n```bash\n--asset-base-url=https://assets.example.com/images\n```\n\nTransforms:\n\n```\nhttps://cdn.sanity.io/images/project/dataset/file.png\nтЖУ\nhttps://assets.example.com/images/file.png\n```\n\n---\n\n# Output\n\nFiles are written to:\n\n```\n/output/\n  webflow-blog-import-<slug>.json\n  webflow-blog-import-<slug>.csv\n```\n\n---\n\n# Webflow Fields Included\n\n## Core Fields\n\n* Name\n* Slug\n* Summary\n* Body (HTML)\n* Author\n* Topics\n* Published Date\n\n## Images\n\n* Featured Image\n* Featured Image Alt\n* Featured Image File Name\n\n## SEO Fields\n\n* SEO Meta Title\n* SEO Meta Description\n* SEO Keywords\n* SEO No Follow\n* SEO Open Graph Title\n* SEO Open Graph Description\n* SEO Open Graph URL\n* SEO Open Graph Site Name\n* SEO Open Graph Image\n* SEO Open Graph Image File Name\n* SEO Twitter Handle\n* SEO Twitter Creator\n* SEO Twitter Site\n* SEO Twitter Card Type\n* SEO Additional Meta Tags JSON\n\n---\n\n# тЪая╕П Important Notes\n\n## 1. assets.json is Required for Filenames\n\nWithout it:\n\n* `data-file-name` will be empty\n* image mapping may break\n\n---\n\n## 2. Relative vs Absolute Image URLs\n\nIf using:\n\n```\n--asset-base-url=/relative-path\n```\n\nMake sure your final site serves images from that path.\n\n---\n\n## 3. Webflow Rich Text Limits\n\nVery large blog posts may exceed Webflow limits.\n\n---\n\n## 4. Unsupported Blocks\n\nSome blocks (e.g. `button-insert`) are exported as placeholders:\n\n```\n[Button Insert module requires manual mapping]\n```\n\n---\n\n# Recommended Workflow\n\n1. Run preview on 1 post\n2. Verify:\n\n   * HTML structure\n   * images\n   * SEO fields\n3. Import into Webflow\n4. Validate rendering\n5. Batch migrate remaining posts\n\n---\n\n# ЁЯПБ Summary\n\nThis script provides a complete pipeline for:\n\n* Sanity тЖТ structured HTML\n* Asset resolution (API + local)\n* SEO flattening\n* Webflow-ready export\n\nIt is designed to be:\n\n* safe (preview mode)\n* flexible (API or local)\n* extensible (custom mappings)\n\n---\n","# Adding or updating redirects in Sanity/Nextjs\n\n---\n\nRedirects are added through Sanity Studio and published via Github Workflow\n\n## Add Redirect in Studio\n\nAdd redirect in Studio > Redirects and Publish:\n\n---\n\n## Create a new branch for the PR\n\nIn GIT, switch to main branch and pull the most recent code from the remote origin\n\n```bash\ngit checkout main\ngit pull origin main\n```\n\nThen create a new branch\n\n```bash\ngit checkout -b update-redirect-name\n```\n\nAdd a comment to the bottom of the next.config file to note the redirect added and have an edit to commit to the branch.\n\n```\n// Redirect added for /some-redirect/\n```\n\nSave and commit:\n\n```bash\ngit add .\ngit commit -m \"Update redirect note\"\n```\n\n## Push the update-redirects branch to remote and create PR\n\nPush the update-redirect-name branch to remote\n\n```bash\ngit push origin update-redirect-name\n```\n\nNext, navigate to Github to create a PR and Merge--this will trigger the Staging workflow--this usually takes 5-10 minutes.\n\nTest redirect in Staging.\n\n\n## Publishing to Prod\n\nUse the Marketing CI workflow to publish main branch to prod:\n\nBranch: main\nDeployment environment: prod\n\nRun Workflow\n\n* workflows can be cancelled in process once they begin running","# GIT command-line instructions for publishing a branch to dev, staging, and prod environments\n\n---\n\nAfter setting up GIT locally and adding remote origin, use these steps for creating a branch and publishing edits to the various environments.\n\n## Create new branch from 'main'\n\nPull updates from repository:\n\n```bash\ngit switch main\ngit pull origin main\n```\n\nThese commands:\n- Switch to the main branch  \n- Pull branch from origin to update \n\n---\n\n## Create new branch and make new edits locally\n\nCreate a branch and run dev locally. Make any edits on this branch and commit changes when finished.\n\n```bash\ngit checkout -b new-branch-name\n```\n\nThis command:\n- Creates a new branch named \"new-branch-name\"\n\n```bash\nnpm run storybook\nnpm run dev\n```\n\nThese commands:\n- Runs Storybook locally for component previewing and development\n- Runs the site locally for development\n\nAfter edits are completed, add files to GIT and commit the changes to the branch.\n\nFirst, double check to make sure you are on the correct branch:\n\n```bash\ngit status\n```\n\nThen:\n\n```bash\ngit status\ngit add .\ngit commit -m \"Message for commit\"\n```\n\nThese commands:\n- Adds all edited files to the commit log\n- Commits the edits to the current branch\n\nNow the branch is ready to push or merge.\n\n\n---\n\n## Publishing to dev (merge)\n\nTo publish to dev, the branch just needs to be merged with the \"dev\" branch. The dev project workflow is set up to publish any time edits are pushed to the dev branch (GitHub Actions). The process usually takes 5-10 minutes to complete once merged.\n\nFirst, once again, switch to the main branch and pull any updates from the remote origin:\n\n```bash\ngit switch main\ngit pull origin main\n```\n\nThen switch to the dev branch and merge the new branch into it. Then push dev to the remote origin to run the workflow:\n\n```bash\ngit switch dev\ngit merge --no-ff new-branch-name -m \"Merge branch-name into dev\"\ngit push origin dev\n```\n\nThese commands:\n- Switch to the dev branch\n- Merges edits from new-branch-name\n- pushes the updated dev branch to the remote origin\n\n### Why use `--no-ff`?\n\n- Preserves branch history\n- Makes feature merges clearly visible in the commit log\n- Cleaner for release tracking\n- Common in team workflows\n\n---\n\n## Publishing to staging\n\nTo publish to staging, the new branch must be pushed to the remote origin and have a Pull Request added. Once the Pull Request is merged with the main branch, the staging publish workflow will be triggered.\n\nSwitch to the new branch or confirm that your are currently on it:\n\n```bash\ngit switch new-branch-name\ngit status\ngit push origin new-branch-name\n```\n\nOnce the branch has been uploaded to the remote repository, navigate to Github and follow the steps to create and merge a pull request...\n\n---\n\n## Publishing to prod\n\nUse the Marketing CI workflow to publish main branch to prod:\n\nBranch: main\nDeployment environment: prod\n\nRun Workflow\n\n* workflows can be cancelled in process once they begin running\n\n\n## Rolling back changes\n\nTo roll back to a previous commit:\n\n- Find the commit hash using\n\n```bash\ngit log --oneline\n```\n\n- THen reset the main brnach using:\n\n```bash\ngit reset --hard <commit-hash>\n```","# Vue.js + Jekyll Overview (Hybrid Static + SPA)\n\n\nThis approach lets you keep **Jekyll** as your static-site generator (posts, pages, layouts) while adding a **Vue 3 single-page app (SPA)** that lives at a dedicated path (e.g. `/app/`) and can consume Jekyll-generated content via JSON. You get:\n\n- **Static Jekyll routes** (SEO-friendly, normal page loads):  \n  `/dev-pages/docs/my-post/`\n- **SPA Vue routes** (dynamic UI, client routing):  \n  `/dev-pages/app/#/post/my-post`\n\nThe key is to use **hash routing** in Vue (`#/...`) so it works on static hosting (GitHub Pages) without server rewrites.\n\n---\n\n## Architecture Options\n\n### 1) Hybrid (Recommended)\n- Jekyll renders the main site + canonical content pages.\n- Vue SPA is mounted on a single Jekyll page (`/app/`).\n- Vue fetches content from a Jekyll-generated JSON feed (`/posts.json`).\n\nтЬЕ Best for: тАЬBoth static pages AND a true SPA in the same repo.тАЭ\n\n### 2) Vue-enhanced Jekyll (Not a full SPA)\n- Keep all routes as Jekyll pages.\n- Use Vue only for components/widgets inside pages.\n\nтЬЕ Best for: interactive UI without client routing.\n\n---\n\n## Successful Process (Brief Outline)\n\n### 1) Configure Jekyll paths\n- Set `baseurl` for GitHub Pages project sites (example):\n  - `baseurl: \"/dev-pages\"`\n- Set permalinks for post URLs (example):\n  - `permalink: /docs/:title/`\n\n### 2) Add a SPA host page in Jekyll\nCreate `/app/` as a real Jekyll page that mounts Vue:\n\n- `app/index.html` тЖТ permalink `/app/`\n- Contains `<div id=\"app\"></div>`\n- Loads the Vue entry module: `assets/app/main.js`\n\nResult:\n- Visiting `/dev-pages/app/` returns real HTML (Jekyll layout + SPA mount point)\n\n### 3) Export Jekyll content as JSON for Vue\nGenerate a JSON feed from `_posts` so the SPA can fetch content:\n\n- `posts.json` with:\n  - `title`, `date`, `slug`, `url`, `content`, etc.\n- Use `relative_url` so Jekyll automatically prefixes `baseurl`\n\nResult:\n- `/dev-pages/posts.json` becomes the SPAтАЩs content API.\n\n### 4) Use Vue 3 + Vue Router with hash routing\nInside `assets/app/main.js`:\n- Use Vue Router with `createWebHashHistory()`\n- Define routes like:\n  - `/` (SPA home)\n  - `/post/:slug` (SPA post view)\n\nResult:\n- SPA routes look like `/dev-pages/app/#/post/my-post`\n- No server rewrite rules required.\n\n### 5) Handle baseurl correctly (critical)\nEnsure fetch calls and static links include the base path:\n\n- Provide baseurl to JS via a global (e.g. `window.__BASEURL__ = \"{{ site.baseurl }}\"`)\n- Fetch JSON using:\n  - `${BASE}/posts.json`\n\nResult:\n- Works the same locally and on GitHub Pages project sites.\n\n### 6) Use import maps or CDN ESM to avoid bundlers (optional but common)\nIf you are not using Vite:\n- Use an `importmap` in `app/index.html` for:\n  - `vue`\n  - `vue-router`\n- Prefer a browser-ready Vue build:\n  - `vue.esm-browser.js`\n\nResult:\n- No Node build step required; the browser can load modules directly.\n\n### 7) Render posts in Vue from the JSON feed\nIn the SPA:\n- Home view fetches `posts.json` and lists items\n- Post view matches `:slug`, finds the post, and renders:\n  - `v-html=\"post.content\"` (Jekyll already converted Markdown тЖТ HTML)\n\nResult:\n- SPA shows the same content as JekyllтАЩs static pages, but with dynamic routing/UI.\n\n---\n\n## Resulting URL Behavior\n\n### Static (Jekyll)\n- `/dev-pages/docs/my-post/` тЖТ full HTML page rendered by Jekyll\n\n### SPA (Vue)\n- `/dev-pages/app/#/post/my-post` тЖТ Vue renders content client-side\n\nThese coexist cleanly because:\n- Jekyll owns `/docs/...`\n- Vue owns hash routes under `/app/`\n\n---\n\n## Recommended Next Steps\n- Add a second feed for metadata-only lists (faster load)\n- Add client-side search (index posts at build time)\n- Add a shared тАЬcanonical linkтАЭ:\n  - SPA page links back to the static Jekyll URL (`post.url`)\n- If the SPA grows, consider switching to Vite for:\n  - bundling, smaller payloads, better DX","[https://docs.github.com/en/repositories/configuring-branches-and-merges-in-your-repository/configuring-pull-request-merges/about-merge-methods-on-github](https://docs.github.com/en/repositories/configuring-branches-and-merges-in-your-repository/configuring-pull-request-merges/about-merge-methods-on-github)\n\n# Adding a Commit Message When Using `git merge`\n\n---\n\n## тЬЕ Basic Syntax\n\n```bash\ngit merge branch-name -m \"Merge branch-name into dev\"\n```\n\nThis command:\n\n- Performs the merge  \n- Uses your provided commit message  \n- Skips opening the default editor (like Vim)  \n\n---\n\n## ЁЯУЭ Multi-line Commit Message\n\nYou can include a commit body by stacking multiple `-m` flags:\n\n```bash\ngit merge branch-name \\\n  -m \"Merge branch-name into dev\" \\\n  -m \"Adds new banner component, styles, and CMS schema updates.\"\n```\n\n- The first `-m` becomes the **commit title**\n- The second `-m` becomes the **commit body**\n\n---\n\n## тЪа Important Notes\n\n- The `-m` flag only applies if Git creates a **merge commit**.\n- If the merge is a **fast-forward**, Git will not create a merge commit тАФ so no message will be used.\n\nIf you want to **force a merge commit** (even when fast-forward is possible), use:\n\n```bash\ngit merge --no-ff branch-name -m \"Merge branch-name into dev\"\n```\n\n### Why use `--no-ff`?\n\n- Preserves branch history\n- Makes feature merges clearly visible in the commit log\n- Cleaner for release tracking\n- Common in team workflows\n\n---\n\n## ЁЯЪА Example: Standard Team Workflow\n\n```bash\ngit checkout dev\ngit pull origin dev\ngit merge --no-ff branch-name -m \"Merge branch-name into dev\"\ngit push origin dev\n```\n\nThis ensures:\n\n1. `dev` is up to date\n2. The merge creates a visible merge commit\n3. The updated branch is pushed to remote\n\n---\n\nIf you'd like, you can also explore:\n\n- Using `--squash` merges\n- Rebasing instead of merging\n- Best practices for CI/CD pipelines","This post was added directly from Github. Once commited, the workfow automatically runs and publishes the new page.\n","[https://tailwindcss.com/docs/installation/framework-guides/nextjs](https://tailwindcss.com/docs/installation/framework-guides/nextjs)\n\n<iframe src=\"https://www.youtube.com/embed/Xb8ajtvFLOk?si=bU8wsFIFT54OVk6B\" title=\"YouTube video player\" frameborder=\"0\" allow=\"accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share\" referrerpolicy=\"strict-origin-when-cross-origin\" allowfullscreen></iframe>\n\n## Tailwind CSS Cheatsheet\n\nA practical reference for common **Tailwind CSS** utility classes (Tailwind v3+).\n\n---\n\n## 1. Layout\n\n### Container\n\n```html\n<div class=\"container mx-auto px-4\"></div>\n```\n\n### Display\n\n```md\nblock | inline-block | inline | flex | inline-flex | grid | hidden\n```\n\n```html\n<div class=\"flex\"></div>\n<div class=\"grid\"></div>\n```\n\n### Position\n\n```md\nstatic | relative | absolute | fixed | sticky\n```\n\n```html\n<div class=\"relative\">\n  <div class=\"absolute top-0 right-0\"></div>\n</div>\n```\n\n### Z-Index\n\n```html\n<div class=\"z-10\"></div>\n```\n\n---\n\n## 2. Flexbox\n\n### Direction & Wrap\n\n```html\n<div class=\"flex flex-row flex-wrap\"></div>\n<div class=\"flex flex-col\"></div>\n```\n\n### Align Items / Justify\n\n```html\n<div class=\"items-start items-center items-end\"></div>\n<div class=\"justify-start justify-center justify-between justify-end\"></div>\n```\n\n### Gap\n\n```html\n<div class=\"gap-2 gap-4 gap-8\"></div>\n```\n\n### Flex Sizing\n\n```html\n<div class=\"flex-1\"></div>\n<div class=\"flex-none\"></div>\n<div class=\"grow\"></div>\n<div class=\"shrink-0\"></div>\n```\n\n---\n\n## 3. Grid\n\n### Columns / Rows\n\n```html\n<div class=\"grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3\"></div>\n<div class=\"grid grid-rows-2\"></div>\n```\n\n### Spans\n\n```html\n<div class=\"col-span-2 row-span-2\"></div>\n```\n\n### Gap\n\n```html\n<div class=\"gap-4\"></div>\n```\n\n---\n\n## 4. Spacing\n\n### Padding / Margin\n\n```html\n<div class=\"p-4 px-6 py-2\"></div>\n<div class=\"m-4 mx-auto my-2\"></div>\n```\n\n### Space Between (children)\n\n```html\n<div class=\"space-y-4\"></div>\n<div class=\"space-x-2\"></div>\n```\n\n---\n\n## 5. Sizing\n\n### Width / Height\n\n```html\n<div class=\"w-full h-screen\"></div>\n<div class=\"w-64 h-48\"></div>\n<div class=\"min-h-screen\"></div>\n<div class=\"max-w-3xl\"></div>\n```\n\n### Aspect Ratio\n\n```html\n<div class=\"aspect-video\"></div>\n```\n\n---\n\n## 6. Typography\n\n### Font Size / Weight\n\n```html\n<p class=\"text-sm text-base text-lg text-xl text-2xl\"></p>\n<p class=\"font-normal font-medium font-semibold font-bold\"></p>\n```\n\n### Line Height / Tracking\n\n```html\n<p class=\"leading-tight leading-normal leading-relaxed\"></p>\n<p class=\"tracking-tight tracking-wide\"></p>\n```\n\n### Alignment\n\n```html\n<p class=\"text-left text-center text-right\"></p>\n```\n\n### Text Transform\n\n```html\n<p class=\"uppercase lowercase capitalize\"></p>\n```\n\n### Truncation\n\n```html\n<p class=\"truncate\"></p>\n<p class=\"line-clamp-2\"></p>\n```\n\n---\n\n## 7. Colors\n\n### Text / Background\n\n```html\n<div class=\"text-gray-900 bg-gray-100\"></div>\n<div class=\"text-white bg-blue-600\"></div>\n```\n\n### Opacity\n\n```html\n<div class=\"bg-black/50\"></div>\n<div class=\"text-white/80\"></div>\n```\n\n---\n\n## 8. Borders & Radius\n\n### Border\n\n```html\n<div class=\"border border-gray-200\"></div>\n<div class=\"border-t border-b\"></div>\n```\n\n### Radius\n\n```html\n<div class=\"rounded rounded-md rounded-lg rounded-xl rounded-2xl\"></div>\n<div class=\"rounded-full\"></div>\n```\n\n---\n\n## 9. Shadows\n\n```html\n<div class=\"shadow shadow-md shadow-lg shadow-xl\"></div>\n```\n\n---\n\n## 10. Backgrounds\n\n### Gradients\n\n```html\n<div class=\"bg-gradient-to-r from-indigo-500 via-purple-500 to-pink-500\"></div>\n```\n\n### Background Image / Cover\n\n```html\n<div class=\"bg-cover bg-center\"></div>\n```\n\n---\n\n## 11. Effects\n\n### Opacity\n\n```html\n<div class=\"opacity-0 opacity-50 opacity-100\"></div>\n```\n\n### Blur\n\n```html\n<div class=\"blur-sm blur-md blur-lg\"></div>\n```\n\n---\n\n## 12. Transitions & Animation\n\n### Transition\n\n```html\n<button class=\"transition duration-200 ease-in-out hover:scale-105\"></button>\n```\n\n### Common\n\n```html\n<div class=\"transition-colors\"></div>\n<div class=\"transition-transform\"></div>\n```\n\n---\n\n## 13. Hover / Focus / Active\n\n```html\n<button class=\"bg-blue-600 hover:bg-blue-700 focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-400 active:scale-95\">\n  Button\n</button>\n```\n\n---\n\n## 14. Responsive Breakpoints\n\n```md\nsm: 640px\nmd: 768px\nlg: 1024px\nxl: 1280px\n2xl: 1536px\n```\n\nExample:\n\n```html\n<div class=\"p-2 md:p-6 lg:p-10\"></div>\n```\n\n---\n\n## 15. Dark Mode\n\n```html\n<div class=\"bg-white text-gray-900 dark:bg-gray-900 dark:text-gray-100\"></div>\n```\n\n---\n\n## 16. State Variants\n\n```md\nhover: focus: active: disabled:\nfirst: last: odd: even:\npeer-*, group-*\n```\n\n### Group Hover\n\n```html\n<div class=\"group\">\n  <span class=\"group-hover:underline\">Hover parent</span>\n</div>\n```\n\n### Peer\n\n```html\n<input class=\"peer\" />\n<p class=\"hidden peer-focus:block\">Shows on focus</p>\n```\n\n---\n\n## 17. Forms\n\n```html\n<input class=\"w-full rounded-md border border-gray-300 px-3 py-2 focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-400\" />\n```\n\n---\n\n## 18. Common UI Patterns\n\n### Card\n\n```html\n<div class=\"rounded-2xl border border-gray-200 bg-white p-6 shadow-md\">\n  <h3 class=\"text-lg font-semibold\">Title</h3>\n  <p class=\"mt-2 text-sm text-gray-600\">Body text</p>\n</div>\n```\n\n### Button\n\n```html\n<button class=\"inline-flex items-center justify-center rounded-md bg-blue-600 px-4 py-2 text-white hover:bg-blue-700 active:scale-95 transition\">\n  Click\n</button>\n```\n\n### Input + Label\n\n```html\n<label class=\"block text-sm font-medium text-gray-700\">Email</label>\n<input class=\"mt-1 w-full rounded-md border border-gray-300 px-3 py-2\" />\n```\n\n---\n\n## 19. Customization\n\n### Arbitrary Values\n\n```html\n<div class=\"w-[420px] top-[3.25rem] bg-[#0f172a]\"></div>\n```\n\n### Extend in tailwind.config\n\n```js\n// theme.extend: colors, spacing, fonts, etc.\n```\n\n---\n\n## 20. Gotchas & Tips\n\n* Prefer `gap-*` over margins for spacing in flex/grid.\n* Use `container mx-auto` for centered layouts.\n* Use `prose` (Typography plugin) for rich text.\n* Use `clsx`/`cn` helper for conditional classes.\n\n---\n\n**Tip:** Build by composing utilities; extract components when repetition shows up.\n","[https://www.markdownguide.org/basic-syntax/](https://www.markdownguide.org/basic-syntax/)\n\n## Markdown Cheatsheet\n\nA quick, practical reference for **writing Markdown** (GitHub-flavored where applicable).\n\n---\n\n## 1. Headings\n\n```md\n# H1\n## H2\n### H3\n#### H4\n##### H5\n###### H6\n```\n\n---\n\n## 2. Text Formatting\n\n```md\n**Bold**\n*Italic*\n***Bold & Italic***\n~~Strikethrough~~\n`Inline code`\n```\n\nResult:\n- **Bold**\n- *Italic*\n- ***Bold & Italic***\n- ~~Strikethrough~~\n- `Inline code`\n\n---\n\n## 3. Paragraphs & Line Breaks\n\n```md\nThis is a paragraph.\n\nThis is another paragraph.\n```\n\nLine break (GitHub-flavored): end a line with two spaces.\n\n---\n\n## 4. Lists\n\n### Unordered\n```md\n- Item\n- Item\n  - Nested item\n```\n\n### Ordered\n```md\n1. First\n2. Second\n3. Third\n```\n\n### Task Lists (GitHub)\n```md\n- [x] Done\n- [ ] Todo\n```\n\n---\n\n## 5. Links\n\n```md\n[Link text](https://example.com)\n```\n\n### Auto Links\n```md\nhttps://example.com\n```\n\n---\n\n## 6. Images\n\n```md\n![Alt text](image.png)\n```\n\n---\n\n## 7. Code Blocks\n\n### Fenced Code Block\n```md\n```js\nconsole.log('Hello');\n```\n```\n\n### Inline Code\n```md\nUse `npm install`\n```\n\n---\n\n## 8. Blockquotes\n\n```md\n> This is a quote\n>> Nested quote\n```\n\n---\n\n## 9. Horizontal Rules\n\n```md\n---\n***\n___\n```\n\n---\n\n## 10. Tables (GitHub)\n\n```md\n| Name | Age | Role |\n|------|-----|------|\n| Sam  | 30  | Dev  |\n| Lee  | 28  | PM   |\n```\nResult:  \n<br>\n| Name | Age | Role |\n|------|-----|------|\n| Sam  | 30  | Dev  |\n| Lee  | 28  | PM   |  \n  \n<br>\n\nAlignment:\n```md\n| Left | Center | Right |\n|:-----|:------:|------:|\n```\n\n---\n\n## 11. Escaping Characters\n\n```md\n\\* \\_ \\# \\`\n```\n\n---\n\n## 12. HTML in Markdown\n\n```md\n<b>Bold</b>\n<br />\n```\n\n---\n\n## 13. Emojis (GitHub)\n\n```md\n:rocket: :sparkles: :bug:\n```\n\n---\n\n## 14. Footnotes (Some Parsers)\n\n```md\nHere is a footnote reference[^1].\n\n[^1]: Footnote text\n```\n\n---\n\n## 15. Collapsible Sections (GitHub)\n\n```md\n<details>\n  <summary>Click to expand</summary>\n\n  Hidden content\n</details>\n```\nResult:  \n<br>\n<details>\n  <summary>Click to expand</summary>\n\n  Hidden content\n</details>  \n<br>\n\n---\n\n## 16. File Extensions\n\n- `.md`\n- `.markdown`\n\n---\n\n## 17. Common Use Cases\n\n- README files\n- Documentation\n- GitHub issues & PRs\n- Notes & wikis\n\n---\n\n## 18. Markdown Gotchas\n\n- Blank lines matter\n- Indentation affects lists & code blocks\n- Not all parsers support all features\n\n---\n\n## 19. GitHub-Flavored Markdown Extras\n\n- Tables\n- Task lists\n- Strikethrough\n- Syntax highlighting\n\n---\n\n**Tip:** Keep Markdown simpleтАФreadability beats clever formatting.\n\n","<!doctype html>\n<html lang=\"en\">\n  <head>\n  <meta charset=\"utf-8\" />\n  <meta http-equiv=\"X-UA-Compatible\" content=\"IE=edge\" />\n\n  <!-- Mobile -->\n  <meta name=\"viewport\" content=\"width=device-width, initial-scale=1\" />\n  <meta name=\"robots\" content=\"noindex, nofollow\">\n\n  <!-- Prevent flash and apply stored/system theme before paint -->\n  <script>\n    (function () {\n      var key = \"theme\";\n      var stored = localStorage.getItem(key);\n      var isDark = stored ? stored === \"dark\" : window.matchMedia(\"(prefers-color-scheme: dark)\").matches;\n      document.documentElement.classList.toggle(\"dark\", isDark);\n      document.documentElement.setAttribute(\"data-theme\", isDark ? \"dark\" : \"light\");\n    })();\n  </script>\n\n  <!-- Title -->\n  <title>\n    \n      Github Pages | Dev-pages\n    \n  </title>\n\n  <link rel=\"stylesheet\" href=\"/dev-pages/assets/css/tailwind.css\">\n</head>\n\n  <body class=\"min-h-screen flex flex-col bg-slate-50 text-slate-900 transition-colors dark:bg-neutral-950 dark:text-neutral-100\">\n\n    <header class=\"bg-white/95 border-b border-slate-200 backdrop-blur dark:bg-neutral-900/95 dark:border-neutral-800\">\n  <div class=\"max-w-7xl mx-auto px-4 py-4 flex justify-between items-center gap-4\">\n    \n    <a href=\"/dev-pages/\" class=\"text-2xl font-bold text-slate-900 hover:text-black transition dark:text-neutral-100 dark:hover:text-white\">\n      Dev-pages\n    </a>\n\n    <div class=\"flex items-center gap-4\">\n      <nav class=\"flex items-center gap-2 text-sm\">\n        \n          <a \n            href=\"/dev-pages/\"\n            class=\"inline-flex items-center rounded-md border border-transparent px-3 py-1.5 text-slate-600 transition hover:text-slate-900 hover:bg-slate-100 dark:text-neutral-400 dark:hover:text-neutral-100 dark:hover:bg-neutral-800\n            \">\n            Home\n          </a>\n        \n          <a \n            href=\"/dev-pages/app/\"\n            class=\"inline-flex items-center rounded-md border border-transparent px-3 py-1.5 text-slate-600 transition hover:text-slate-900 hover:bg-slate-100 dark:text-neutral-400 dark:hover:text-neutral-100 dark:hover:bg-neutral-800\n            \">\n            Docs\n          </a>\n        \n          <a \n            href=\"/dev-pages/about/\"\n            class=\"inline-flex items-center rounded-md border border-transparent px-3 py-1.5 text-slate-600 transition hover:text-slate-900 hover:bg-slate-100 dark:text-neutral-400 dark:hover:text-neutral-100 dark:hover:bg-neutral-800\n            \">\n            About\n          </a>\n        \n          <a \n            href=\"/dev-pages/contact/\"\n            class=\"inline-flex items-center rounded-md border border-transparent px-3 py-1.5 text-slate-600 transition hover:text-slate-900 hover:bg-slate-100 dark:text-neutral-400 dark:hover:text-neutral-100 dark:hover:bg-neutral-800\n            \">\n            Contact\n          </a>\n        \n      </nav>\n\n      <button\n        id=\"theme-toggle\"\n        type=\"button\"\n        class=\"inline-flex items-center rounded-md border border-slate-300 px-3 py-1.5 text-xs font-semibold text-slate-700 hover:bg-slate-100 transition dark:border-neutral-700 dark:text-neutral-200 dark:hover:bg-neutral-800\"\n        aria-label=\"Toggle theme\"\n      >\n        <span class=\"hidden dark:block\"><!-- _includes/icon.html -->\n\n<svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" fill=\"currentColor\" class=\"bi bi-brightness-high\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\">\n  <path d=\"M8 11a3 3 0 1 1 0-6 3 3 0 0 1 0 6m0 1a4 4 0 1 0 0-8 4 4 0 0 0 0 8M8 0a.5.5 0 0 1 .5.5v2a.5.5 0 0 1-1 0v-2A.5.5 0 0 1 8 0m0 13a.5.5 0 0 1 .5.5v2a.5.5 0 0 1-1 0v-2A.5.5 0 0 1 8 13m8-5a.5.5 0 0 1-.5.5h-2a.5.5 0 0 1 0-1h2a.5.5 0 0 1 .5.5M3 8a.5.5 0 0 1-.5.5h-2a.5.5 0 0 1 0-1h2A.5.5 0 0 1 3 8m10.657-5.657a.5.5 0 0 1 0 .707l-1.414 1.415a.5.5 0 1 1-.707-.708l1.414-1.414a.5.5 0 0 1 .707 0m-9.193 9.193a.5.5 0 0 1 0 .707L3.05 13.657a.5.5 0 0 1-.707-.707l1.414-1.414a.5.5 0 0 1 .707 0m9.193 2.121a.5.5 0 0 1-.707 0l-1.414-1.414a.5.5 0 0 1 .707-.707l1.414 1.414a.5.5 0 0 1 0 .707M4.464 4.465a.5.5 0 0 1-.707 0L2.343 3.05a.5.5 0 1 1 .707-.707l1.414 1.414a.5.5 0 0 1 0 .708\"/>\n</svg></span>\n        <span class=\"block dark:hidden\"><!-- _includes/icon.html -->\n\n<svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" fill=\"currentColor\" class=\"bi bi-moon\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\">\n  <path d=\"M6 .278a.77.77 0 0 1 .08.858 7.2 7.2 0 0 0-.878 3.46c0 4.021 3.278 7.277 7.318 7.277q.792-.001 1.533-.16a.79.79 0 0 1 .81.316.73.73 0 0 1-.031.893A8.35 8.35 0 0 1 8.344 16C3.734 16 0 12.286 0 7.71 0 4.266 2.114 1.312 5.124.06A.75.75 0 0 1 6 .278M4.858 1.311A7.27 7.27 0 0 0 1.025 7.71c0 4.02 3.279 7.276 7.319 7.276a7.32 7.32 0 0 0 5.205-2.162q-.506.063-1.029.063c-4.61 0-8.343-3.714-8.343-8.29 0-1.167.242-2.278.681-3.286\"/>\n</svg></span>\n      </button>\n    </div>\n\n  </div>\n</header>\n\n\n    <div class=\"w-full flex-1 max-w-7xl mx-auto px-4 py-10 flex\">\n      <div class=\"grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-4 gap-10 flex-1 items-stretch\">\n\n        <!-- Sidebar -->\n        <aside class=\"md:col-span-1 h-full\">\n          <div class=\"sticky top-8\">\n\n  <h2 class=\"text-sm font-semibold uppercase tracking-wide text-slate-500 mb-4 dark:text-neutral-500\">\n    Docs\n  </h2>\n\n  <ul class=\"space-y-3 text-sm\">\n    \n      <li>\n        <a \n          href=\"/dev-pages/docs/sanity-to-webflow-blog/\"\n          class=\"block text-slate-600 hover:text-slate-900 transition dark:text-neutral-400 dark:hover:text-neutral-100\n          \">\n          Sanity to Webflow - blog\n        </a>\n      </li>\n    \n      <li>\n        <a \n          href=\"/dev-pages/docs/redirects/\"\n          class=\"block text-slate-600 hover:text-slate-900 transition dark:text-neutral-400 dark:hover:text-neutral-100\n          \">\n          Update Redirects\n        </a>\n      </li>\n    \n      <li>\n        <a \n          href=\"/dev-pages/docs/publishing/\"\n          class=\"block text-slate-600 hover:text-slate-900 transition dark:text-neutral-400 dark:hover:text-neutral-100\n          \">\n          Publishing Processes\n        </a>\n      </li>\n    \n      <li>\n        <a \n          href=\"/dev-pages/docs/jekyll-vuejs-spa/\"\n          class=\"block text-slate-600 hover:text-slate-900 transition dark:text-neutral-400 dark:hover:text-neutral-100\n          \">\n          Jekyll + Vuejs SPA\n        </a>\n      </li>\n    \n      <li>\n        <a \n          href=\"/dev-pages/docs/github-merges/\"\n          class=\"block text-slate-600 hover:text-slate-900 transition dark:text-neutral-400 dark:hover:text-neutral-100\n          \">\n          Github Merges\n        </a>\n      </li>\n    \n      <li>\n        <a \n          href=\"/dev-pages/docs/testpost/\"\n          class=\"block text-slate-600 hover:text-slate-900 transition dark:text-neutral-400 dark:hover:text-neutral-100\n          \">\n          My New Post\n        </a>\n      </li>\n    \n      <li>\n        <a \n          href=\"/dev-pages/docs/tailwind/\"\n          class=\"block text-slate-600 hover:text-slate-900 transition dark:text-neutral-400 dark:hover:text-neutral-100\n          \">\n          Tailwind\n        </a>\n      </li>\n    \n      <li>\n        <a \n          href=\"/dev-pages/docs/markdown/\"\n          class=\"block text-slate-600 hover:text-slate-900 transition dark:text-neutral-400 dark:hover:text-neutral-100\n          \">\n          Markdown\n        </a>\n      </li>\n    \n      <li>\n        <a \n          href=\"/dev-pages/docs/jekyll/\"\n
